title: Engage LILYGO on T-Echo GNSS PPS
description: Establish whether the current LilyGO T-Echo exposes the L76K 1PPS signal to the nRF52840 and, if not, explore a manufacturer-supported revision that does. The commercial objective is a LICHEN reference radio combining SX1262, e-ink, GNSS position, and hardware-captured PPS for optional high-density scheduled multichannel operation. Keep baseline CAD/CSMA compatibility explicit and make no purchase, MOQ, exclusivity, branding, or custom-hardware commitment without separate authorization.
design: Sequence the work from facts to evidence to relationship: verify current schematic/PCB; prepare a concise engineering request and measured TDMA value case; obtain explicit authorization before contacting LILYGO; ask first for pin-routing confirmation and engineering access, not a custom order; evaluate responses, sample/revision options, NRE/MOQ/lead time, and openness of board support; then validate PPS electrically and in firmware. The minimum useful change is L76K 1PPS routed to an nRF52840 GPIOTE-capable GPIO suitable for PPI timer capture, with a production test point and documented voltage, polarity, pulse width, fixed delay, and jitter. Prefer exposing GNSS EXTINT/wake and power control when available, but do not expand scope if PPS alone is feasible.
acceptance_criteria: Current T-Echo PPS connectivity is established from manufacturer-grade evidence; the exact technical questions, rationale, and non-commitment language are prepared; authorized outreach receives a durable response or a documented no-response cadence; any proposed revision records pin choice, electrical characteristics, test point, GNSS power/wake behavior, schematic/board-support availability, samples, NRE, MOQ, unit pricing, and lead time; physical samples demonstrate hardware timer capture, GPS-second association, jitter/offset characterization, holdover fallback, and measurable scheduled-mode guard/capacity improvement; the 500-node demo plan is corrected if PPS is unavailable or uneconomic.
notes: Research result project-LICHEN-2nnd.5.2: official T-Echo/Plus design already routes GNSS 1PPS through 1 kOhm to nRF52840 P1.04. Manufacturer engagement should no longer lead with a board-change request. Ask LILYGO to confirm PPS connectivity across current production revisions/batches, identify GNSS substitutions, guarantee or document P1.04 behavior, provide pulse timing/jitter/fixed-delay data and production test coverage, and discuss a labeled test point only as an optional future improvement. Physical precision/CCP validation remains required.
